key_insight: PriEco-DRL jointly coordinates electric-bus eco-driving and transit-priority adaptive signals with deep reinforcement learning to reduce energy use while preserving reliability and comfort.
engineering_relevance: The framework offers a practical way to tune the energy–time trade-off in real transit corridors, helping operators balance bus efficiency, priority service, and network performance.
method: Priority-weighted max-pressure signal control allocates green time using occupancy-aware pressure.
CTDE with parameter sharing lets one DRL agent learn from multiple buses/routes using local observations.
A structured reward blends guidance and event-based reinforcement over energy, time, comfort, and safety.
evidence_signal: Experiments on a real-world corridor show lower electric-bus energy consumption than fixed-time, actuated, and rule-based baselines while maintaining transit priority and network efficiency.
watch_item: Performance depends on reward weighting and local signal uncertainty, so careful tuning and field validation are needed before deployment.
paper_title: PriEco-DRL: Joint Optimization of Electric-Bus Eco-Driving and Transit-Priority Adaptive Signals via Deep Reinforcement Learning
